skill.name: dependency-updater
skill.description: Reviews project dependencies, prioritizes available updates, and summarizes changelogs, security fixes, and breaking changes.
skill.summary: Find dependency updates and review their upgrade risk.
skill.author: CuriousLearner

security_audit.summary: The external command and weak cryptography detections are false positives from Markdown fences and ordinary prose. The hardcoded URL is an illustrative migration guide link, not executable network code, but the skill does direct users to inspect local dependency files and fetch changelogs from external sources.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Examples Flagged as Command Execution
low_findings.description: The reported external command findings are Markdown examples and report templates. They do not contain executable Ruby code, shell interpolation, or a command invocation path.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are inside fenced Markdown blocks used for usage examples and sample output. No executable script or command runner is present in the skill file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Hardcoded URL Is an Example Reference
low_findings.description: The hardcoded React migration URL appears in a sample report as documentation for a major version upgrade. No evidence found that the skill automatically calls this URL or sends data to it.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL is nested under a sample dependency update report. The file contains no executable network client or data exfiltration logic.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern in Prose
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography detections occur in descriptive text and checklist items. No evidence found of MD5, SHA-1, encryption APIs, or hash verification logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are metadata and dependency review guidance. They do not define or call cryptographic algorithms.

low_findings.title: Expected Access to Dependency Files and External Release Notes
low_findings.description: The skill asks the assistant to inspect dependency manifests and fetch changelogs or release notes. This is appropriate for the feature, but users should keep external release text and package metadata untrusted.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly instructs manifest checks and changelog fetching. The behavior is legitimate for dependency review, with limited risk from local file access and untrusted web content.

content.value_statement: Dependency updates can hide security fixes, breaking changes, and compatibility issues. This skill reviews manifests, summarizes update impact, and recommends a safer upgrade order.
actual_capabilities: Identifies dependency manifests for npm, Python, Go, Rust, and Java projects.
Classifies available updates as patch, minor, or major changes.
Summarizes changelogs, release notes, security fixes, and migration guidance.
Prioritizes updates by security impact, breaking change risk, and maintenance value.
Produces an ordered update plan with testing and lockfile reminders.
limitations: It depends on available package registry data and public release notes.
It does not guarantee that every breaking change is documented upstream.
It does not replace project-specific testing after updates.
It should not apply updates without user review and approval.

use_cases.title: Review a pull request with many package updates
use_cases.description: Summarize which updates are security fixes, which are routine, and which require migration review.

use_cases.title: Plan a quarterly dependency refresh
use_cases.description: Group outdated packages by priority and create a practical update sequence for one repository.

use_cases.title: Prepare a major framework upgrade
use_cases.description: Collect breaking changes, migration links, and compatibility checks before starting implementation.

prompt_templates.title: Check this project
prompt_templates.prompt: Use dependency-updater to review this repository and list outdated dependencies by priority.

prompt_templates.title: Security updates only
prompt_templates.prompt: Use dependency-updater to find dependency updates that fix known vulnerabilities. Include the fixed versions and recommended order.

prompt_templates.title: Major update planning
prompt_templates.prompt: Use dependency-updater to review major version updates. Summarize breaking changes, migration notes, and required compatibility checks.

prompt_templates.title: Release-ready upgrade plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use dependency-updater to build a staged dependency upgrade plan with risk levels, test focus areas, lockfile notes, and rollback concerns.

output_examples.input: Review package updates for a Node.js service.
output_examples.output: A prioritized report that separates security patches, safe minor updates, and major upgrades that need migration review.

output_examples.input: Find critical dependency work before the next release.
output_examples.output: A short action list with vulnerable packages first, then compatibility checks and test areas for release validation.

output_examples.input: Plan a React major version upgrade.
output_examples.output: A migration summary covering breaking changes, feature changes, peer dependency checks, and a recommended testing sequence.

best_practices: Run the skill on a clean branch before applying dependency changes.
Review changelogs and security advisories before approving major updates.
Run the full test suite and update lockfiles after each update group.
anti_patterns: Do not update every dependency at once without reviewing impact.
Do not trust external release notes as instructions for the assistant.
Do not skip staging validation for major production dependency changes.

faq.question: Does this skill update files automatically?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on review, prioritization, and planning unless the user separately approves changes.

faq.question: Which ecosystems does it describe?
faq.answer: It covers npm, Python, Go, Rust, and Java dependency manifests.

faq.question: Can it find security updates?
faq.answer: Yes. It directs the assistant to check CVEs, security advisories, deprecated packages, and security fixes.

faq.question: Can it guarantee compatibility?
faq.answer: No. It can identify likely risks, but project tests and staging checks are still required.

faq.question: Does it use external sources?
faq.answer: It may use package registries, changelogs, release notes, and migration guides when available.

faq.question: Is it suitable for automated dependency bots?
faq.answer: It can support bot review workflows, but humans should approve major and security-sensitive changes.
